overall_assessment.fls_score_context: Your 464.5 score represents solid intermediate-level performance, held back primarily by time (147s) rather than technique errors.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Excellent knot technique with proper surgeon's knot and hand switching - focus now shifts to building speed and placement precision.

rubric_insights.observation: 147-second completion time cost significant points compared to your faster sessions (42-45s range)
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Approximately 80-100 points lost to time penalties compared to your peak performances

rubric_insights.observation: 2.5mm total deviation from marks resulted in minor point deduction
Perfect knot sequence - surgeon's knot followed by alternating single throws with proper hand switching
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full points earned for knot technique

technique_coaching.observation: 41-second cutting phase (frames 21 completion vs 106s cut start) suggests excessive deliberation or repositioning during final steps
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Cutting efficiency directly impacts total time and is often the easiest phase to accelerate without compromising safety
technique_coaching.correction: Approach scissors to knot immediately after final throw tightening, maintain 2-3mm distance from knot, make decisive single cut
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 rapid cuts on practice knots - load scissors, approach knot, cut within 3 seconds per attempt

technique_coaching.observation: 12-second needle loading phase indicates room for efficiency gains in initial setup
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Fast, confident needle loading sets the rhythm for the entire task and builds momentum
technique_coaching.correction: Load needle at 2/3 point from tip in single motion, avoid repositioning once grasped
technique_coaching.drill: Needle loading speed drill: 15 repetitions loading needle in under 5 seconds each

technique_coaching.observation: Frame 6 shows educational note about 'wrong traction' - suggests some tension management refinement needed
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Proper traction prevents knot slippage and ensures secure closure without tissue damage
technique_coaching.correction: Apply steady, even tension perpendicular to tissue surface during knot advancement
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 10 knots focusing solely on smooth, consistent traction without jerky movements

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Excellent knot sequence execution with proper surgeon's knot initiation and consistent hand switching pattern
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: This demonstrates mastery of the fundamental FLS knot requirements and will serve you well in advanced procedures
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot technique is textbook perfect - this foundation will accelerate your progress as you build speed

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Good suture placement accuracy with only 2.5mm total deviation from target marks
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Accurate placement shows good spatial awareness and instrument control
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your placement precision is already at competent level - small refinements will push you to expert range

practice_plan.session_focus: Cutting phase efficiency - reducing 41-second cutting time to under 15 seconds
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of rapid needle loading practice (target: under 5 seconds per load)

primary_drill.name: Speed Cutting Challenge
primary_drill.description: Complete full Task 5, but focus intensely on cutting phase. After final throw: (1) Immediately position scissors, (2) Approach knot at 2-3mm distance, (3) Make single decisive cut, (4) Withdraw. Time only the cutting phase.
primary_drill.success_criteria: Cutting phase under 15 seconds with clean cut 2-3mm from knot

practice_plan.cooldown: Review your fastest cutting attempt - what felt different about the efficient ones?
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 2-3 focused sessions should bring total time under 120 seconds

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early 180s times, but current performance below your 42-45s peak range
progress_context.vs_peak: About 100 seconds slower than your best performances (42-45s range)
progress_context.milestone_note: Target: Sub-120s with current knot quality maintained

coaching_limitations: Camera angle limits assessment of exact needle loading grip position and scissors approach angle during cutting phase
